title: Some customer phone numbers are wrong in TaskFlow
body: We noticed that some of our customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers. The numbers look truncated or mangled. Our support team caught it because they tried calling a few customers and got wrong numbers. We have about 2,000 customer records and I'd estimate maybe 50-100 have wrong numbers. This is a serious data integrity issue for us.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — data integrity issues like this are serious and we want to get to the bottom of it.

First question: do you know approximately **when** these phone numbers went wrong? Specifically:

- Were these records correct at some point and then became corrupted (i.e., you could previously call these numbers successfully)?
- Or is it possible they were never stored correctly in the first place — for example, after a bulk import, a CSV upload, or a migration?

This will help us figure out whether we're looking at data corruption of existing records vs. a bug in how numbers are entered or imported.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yes, these numbers were definitely correct before. Our support team has been calling these customers for months without issues. I think the problem started maybe 2 weeks ago? We do have a nightly import that syncs data from our Salesforce CRM into TaskFlow, so it's possible something went wrong there. I didn't think about it until you asked, but the timing might line up with when the numbers started going bad.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the nightly Salesforce sync is a strong lead. Let's dig into what the corruption actually looks like.

Can you pull up 2-3 of the affected records and share what the phone number looks like **in TaskFlow** vs. what it should be (or what it shows in Salesforce)? For example:

- Customer A: TaskFlow shows `555-123`, Salesforce shows `+1 (555) 123-4567`

The specific pattern of how the numbers are wrong will tell us a lot — whether digits are being truncated, whether formatting characters are being stripped, whether country codes are being dropped, etc.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Sure, I pulled up a few that our support team flagged. Here's what I'm seeing:

- Customer with an Ireland number: Salesforce has +353 21 234 5678, but TaskFlow shows +35 21 234 5678. Looks like a digit got chopped off the front part.
- Customer from Antigua: Salesforce has +1268 555 1234, TaskFlow shows +12 555 1234. That's really mangled.
- I also checked a US customer just to compare: Salesforce has +1 555 867 5309 and TaskFlow shows the same thing, so that one's fine.

It does look like something is cutting off part of the number, but only on some of them. I'm not sure why some are affected and others aren't.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
I think I can see the pattern. Looking at your examples:

- Ireland `+353` → `+35` — the 3-digit country code got cut to 2 digits
- Antigua `+1268` → `+12` — the 4-digit country code got cut to 2 digits
- US `+1` — unaffected because the country code is already 1 digit

It looks like the nightly Salesforce sync is truncating country codes to a maximum of 2 digits. Does that match what you're seeing across the other ~50-100 affected records? Specifically — are the affected customers **all** in countries with 3+ digit country codes (e.g., countries outside North America and a handful of others with short codes)?

Also: was there any change to the Salesforce sync configuration, the TaskFlow phone number schema, or any related update roughly 2 weeks ago? Even a routine update or dependency upgrade could have introduced a new phone number validation or formatting step.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Yeah, that pattern makes sense now that you point it out. I just spot-checked a few more and they do all seem to be international numbers — I see ones from India, some Caribbean countries, that kind of thing. The US and UK numbers all look fine. So I think you're right that it's the longer country codes getting chopped.

As for changes around that time — I'm not sure about anything on the TaskFlow side specifically, but I do remember getting a notification or changelog note about some update to how phone numbers are handled? Something about international format support I think. I don't remember the exact details but it was roughly around when this started. You'd probably need to check with whoever manages that on your end.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Nightly Salesforce sync truncates international phone country codes to 2 digits","problem":"Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer phone numbers have been corrupted by the nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow sync. Country codes longer than 2 digits are being truncated to exactly 2 digits, mangling international phone numbers while leaving short-code countries (US +1, UK +44) unaffected.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A recent update (~2 weeks ago) to the phone number handling or international format support in the Salesforce sync pipeline introduced a bug that truncates the country code portion of phone numbers to a maximum of 2 digits. This likely occurs in a parsing or normalization step that incorrectly assumes all country codes are 1-2 digits.","reproduction_steps":["Create or identify a customer record in Salesforce with a 3+ digit country code (e.g., Ireland +353 21 234 5678)","Wait for the nightly sync to run (or trigger it manually)","Check the phone number in TaskFlow — the country code should be truncated to 2 digits (e.g., +35 21 234 5678)","Verify a US (+1) number syncs correctly as a control case"],"environment":"Nightly Salesforce CRM → TaskFlow sync pipeline. Issue started approximately 2 weeks ago, coinciding with a reported update to phone number / international format handling.","severity":"high","impact":"Support team cannot reach ~50-100 international customers by phone. Data integrity of customer contact records is compromised. Affected customers are exclusively those in countries with 3+ digit country codes (e.g., Ireland, India, Caribbean nations). Each nightly sync may be re-corrupting any manually corrected records.","recommended_fix":"1. Check the Salesforce sync code's phone number parsing/normalization logic for a recent change (~2 weeks ago) that handles country codes — look for a substring, regex, or field-length constraint that limits the country code to 2 characters. 2. Fix the parser to support country codes of 1-3 digits per the ITU E.164 standard. 3. Run a one-time remediation: re-sync all phone numbers from Salesforce (the source of truth) to TaskFlow to restore corrupted records. 4. Consider adding a validation check that flags phone numbers whose country code doesn't match a known valid code.","proposed_test_case":"Unit test the phone number normalization function with inputs covering 1-digit (+1 US), 2-digit (+44 UK), 3-digit (+353 Ireland), and combined country+area codes that look long (+1268 Antigua). Assert that the full country code is preserved in all cases and the resulting number matches E.164 format.","information_gaps":["Exact changelog entry or commit that introduced the phone number handling change ~2 weeks ago (discoverable from version control)","Whether manually corrected records are being re-corrupted on each nightly sync cycle","Whether phone numbers entered directly in TaskFlow (not via sync) are also affected by the same normalization bug"]}
